title: What Really Improves Mathematical Reasoning: Structured Reasoning Signals Beyond Pure Code
summary:
arXiv:2605.19762v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Code has become a standard component of modern foundation language model (LM) training, yet its role beyond programming remains unclear. We revisit the claim that code improves reasoning through controlled pretraining experiments on a 10T-token corpus with fine-grained domain separation. Our findings are threefold.
